name: sled
description: Lock-free, pure Rust embedded database with BTreeMap-like API and ACID transactions

description.value: Lock-free, pure Rust embedded database with BTreeMap-like API and ACID transactions
description.comment: Self-described as 'the champagne of beta embedded databases'

first-release.comment: Originally created as 'rsdb' in 2016, renamed to sled in 2017

schema-model.comment: Raw byte-oriented API similar to BTreeMap<[u8], [u8]>

transactions.comment: ACID-compliant serializable transactions across multiple keyspaces

range-queries.comment: Ordered key-value store with iterator operations

in-memory-mode.comment: Persistent storage only, no dedicated in-memory mode

storage-architecture.comment: LLAMA-based pagecache with scatter-gather page materialization, optimized for SSDs

encryption-at-rest.comment: No native encryption at rest support

memory-mapped-io.comment: Uses custom pagecache instead of mmap

max-db-size.comment: 32-bit: ~4B pages limit; 64-bit: significantly higher. Database on 64-bit may not be valid on 32-bit

pure-rust.comment: 100% pure Rust with no C/C++ dependencies

async-support.comment: Primarily synchronous API, though has flush_async and Future-based subscriptions

api-safety.comment: Public API is safe Rust, lock-free internally

idiomatic-api.comment: BTreeMap-like API is familiar to Rust developers, though byte-oriented keys/values require manual serialization

type-safety.comment: Raw [u8] interface, no built-in type safety

serde-integration.comment: No native serde integration, users must serialize manually

wasm-compatible.comment: Not compatible with WASM due to file I/O requirements

acid-compliant.comment: Full ACID transaction support with serializable isolation

crash-recovery.comment: Automatic fsync every 500ms (configurable) with crash recovery

multi-process.comment: Single process per database instance only

maturity.comment: Explicitly beta status. README states: 'if reliability is your primary constraint, use SQLite'

ios.comment: Described as usable on 'servers and phones'

android.comment: Described as usable on 'servers and phones'

use-cases.comment: Best for stateful services and systems requiring embedded persistence with high concurrency

maintenance-status.comment: Last release 2021, though author is working on komora/marble rewrite. Limited recent activity beyond dependabot updates

documentation-quality.comment: Good API documentation and examples, but some features need more explanation

bus-factor.comment: Tyler Neely (spacejam) is the primary maintainer
